channel.title: dotnet/winforms Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/winforms, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Fix build - @RussKie
item.description:
Remove unnecessary using

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Fixing freezing ListView in virtual mode when Accessibility Tool is active - @SergeySmirnov-Akvelon
item.description:
Fixes #5033

Proposed changes

  • The issue is reproduced because each time we get the Accessibility object, we calculate the current index of the ListViewItemAccessibleObject and the index to get the next / previous ListViewItem. Both operations use a loop that checks all the ListViewItem in order to find the one we want. Unfortunately, when we have 1 million ListViewItem, this approach becomes too laborious.
  • Fixed the logic for getting indices, now we use the element's index instead of calculating it every time

Customer Impact

Before fix:

After fix:

Regression?

  • Yes

Risk

  • Minimal

Test methodology

  • Manually
  • CTI team

Accessibility testing

  • Narrator
  • Inspect
  • Accessibility Insights

Test environment(s)

  • Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19041.388]
  • .NET Core SDK: 6.0.0-preview.7.21352.2
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Clean up PropertyGrid entries - @JeremyKuhne
item.description:
Primarily focusing on local variable names for this iteration.

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Fixing Entry not found exception for AdjustWindowRectExForDpi(). (#5260) - @dreddy-work
item.description:
Proposed changes

  • We recently fixed an issue to support a high DPI scenario that was using the latest Windows API. however, this API was introduced in Windows 10 (version >1607) and was not serviced/down ported back to Windows7. As a result, running Winforms applications targeting .NET 6.0 on Windows 7 are throwing Entry not found exception for API AdjustWindowRectExForDpi(). This change is checking for the OS at runtime before calling this API.

Customer Impact

  • Customers running .NET 6.0 Winforms applications on Windows 7 with latest .NET 6.0 preview installed.

Regression?

  • From recent .NET 6.0 preview (6).

Risk

  • Minimal
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Porting to Preview7: Fixing Entry not found exception for AdjustWindowRectExForDpi(). (#5260) - @dreddy-work
item.description:
Proposed changes

  • We recently fixed an issue to support an high DPI scenario that was using the latest Windows API. however, this API was introduced in Windows 10 (version >1607) and was not serviced/down ported back to Windows7. As a result, running Winforms applications targetting .NET 6.0 on Windows 7 are throwing Entry not found exception for API AdjustWindowRectExForDpi(). This change is checking for the OS at runtime before calling this API.

Customer Impact

  • Customers running .NET 6.0 Winforms applications on Windows 7 with latest .NET 6.0 preview installed.

Regression?

  • From recent .NET 6.0 preview (6).

Risk

  • Minimal
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Implementing support UIA provider for CheckedListBox control - @DmitryGorokhov
item.description:
Related Issue: #3421

Proposed changes

  • Switched on SupportsUiaProviders property of CheckedListBox class.
  • Updated controls tree and navigation.
  • Updating uia properties (in progress) ...
  • Adding unit tests (in progress) ...
  • Control checkedListBox1 was placed on ListView form.

Customer Impact

  • Improving development experience for CheckedListBox control accessibility.

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • Minimal

Screenshots

Before

After

Test methodology

item.title: PR: Fixing Entry not found exception for AdjustWindowRectExForDpi(). - @dreddy-work
item.description:
Making sure we call AdjustWindowRectExForDpi only on supported OS. This API is available in Windows 10 and above versions.

Fixes : https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/unable-to-find-an-entry-point-named-Adj/1476945?entry=problem&space=61&q=AdjustWindowRectEx&viewtype=all

related #4797

fixes #5278

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Add extensions for ComponentChanging/Changed - @JeremyKuhne
item.description:
Add extensions to allow providing default values. Clean up some code around usages. Move service extensions up in namespace to System.

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Add ServiceProvider extensions - @JeremyKuhne
item.description:
Add ServiceProvider extensions from the designer code. For try methods allow for null provider. Add internal overloads to Control.

Annotate PropertyTab and use the new overloads in some of the PropertyGrid classes.

Handle unexpected/null ISite.

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Publishing APIs for current preview. - @dreddy-work
item.description:
Publishing Unshipped APIs for the upcoming preview.

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Adding support expand-collapse pattern for DateTimePicker - @DmitryGorokhov
item.description:
Implemented expand-collapse pattern in DateTimePickerAccessibleObject class

Fixes #4099

Proposed changes

  • Implemented expand-collapse pattern in DateTimePickerAccessibleObject class
  • Added unit tests

Customer Impact

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • Minimal

Screenshots

Before

  • In the Accessibility Insight tool, an error 'An element of the given ControlType must support the ExpandCollapse pattern' appeared.

  • In the inspect tool, the IsExpandCollapsePattermAvaliable property value is false.

After

  • In the Accessibility Insight tool, no error 'An element of the given ControlType must support the ExpandCollapse pattern' appeared.

  • In the inspect tool, the IsExpandCollapsePattermAvaliable property value is true and the ExpandCollapseExpandCollapseState property appear.

  • Updated notification in narrator.

Test methodology

  • Manually test
  • Unit test
  • Accessibility test

Accessibility testing

  • Inspect
  • Narrator
  • Accessibility insight

Test environment(s)

  • .NET SDK: 6.0.0-preview.7.21365.2
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Fixing color contrast for CheckedListBox - @SergeySmirnov-Akvelon
item.description:
Fixes #3059

Proposed changes

  • During investigation, we found that the orange color from the issue is obtained by inverting the blue (highlighted) color. Unfortunately, orange does not contrast well with most colors. Therefore, during the discussions, it was decided to change the orange color to white. Thus, we get a black and white dotted border that has the necessary contrast with the blue (highlighted) color and the colors that the user can set.
  • Added "DrawBlackWhiteFocusRectangle" method.
  • Updated parameters for "DrawFocusRectangle" method.
  • Added additional logic to the "GetFocusPen" method to get the required border colors.

Customer Impact

Before fix:

After fix:

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • Minimal

Test methodology

  • Manually

Accessibility testing

  • Accessibility Insights

Test environment(s)

  • Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19041.388]
  • .NET Core SDK: 6.0.0-preview.7.21363.9
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Clean up PropertyGrid/View variable names - @JeremyKuhne
item.description:
Mostly changing the local and member variables to be more in line with current styles. Some general code cleanup as well.

This doesn't fix everything, but is another step towards readability.

item.title: PR: Fix which prevent usage of debug builds in NativeAOT - @kant2002
item.description:
Only changes is Enum.GetValues() to Enum.GetValue<TEnum>(). Other changes is just to make compiler happy. This change affects even when compile in reflection mode, I see other opportinities where reflection can be trimmed.

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Prevented unit tests failures [part 2] (#5223) - @OlegRa
item.description:
Fixes #5223 (completely)

Proposed changes

  • The SystemInformation.MenuAccessKeyUnderlined property used as expected value for checking Control.ShowKeyboardCues and Control.ShowFocusCues default values for newly created controls in unit tests.

Customer Impact

  • None (unit-tests)

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • None

Test methodology

  • Run unit tests on PC with the "Underline access keys when available" option enabled and disabled.

Test environment(s)

  • .NET 6.0.100-preview.6.21317.3
  • Windows 10 settings - "Ease of Access" -> "Keyboard" -> "Change how keybord shortcuts work"
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Merging internal commits for release/5.0 - @vseanreesermsft
item.description:
null

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Adding "IsAccessibilityObjectCreated" property - @SergeySmirnov-Akvelon
item.description:
Proposed changes

  • Added "IsAccessibilityObjectCreated" property
  • The "IsAccessibilityObjectCreated" property is needed to get data about whether an accessibility object has been created. Currently, we only have access to the accessibility object through the "AccessibilityObject" property, which forces the accessibility object to be created when we try to access it. The "IsAccessibilityObjectCreated" property will help avoid using memory by unnecessary accessibility objects and prevent execution of unnecessary code when external accessibility tools are not active

Customer Impact

  • No

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • Minimal

Test environment(s)

  • Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19041.388]
  • .NET Core SDK: 6.0.0-preview.7.21352.2
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Possibility to align minimized MDI child to default location (TOP LEFT) - @kirsan31
item.description:
Fix https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/issues/4720.

Proposed changes

  • Add public bool property MdiChildrenMinimizedAnchorBottom to Form class.

Customer Impact

WinForms override standard MDI behavior without option to use original one. It's lead to all child windows layout (with minimized windows) are mess up after main window resize. This can happened after simple resize, or after resolution changed (for example if your connect by rdp from other device), etc... Customer can use MdiChildrenMinimizedAnchorBottom to revert for Windows default behavior.

Regression?

No

Risk

Minimal.

Screenshots

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17767561/125053862-3e5ac700-e0ae-11eb-8ff1-3b5f72500f9d.mp4

P.s. Video above with old name of the property.

Test methodology

Manual. Maui automated test.

Accessibility testing

Unfortunately, I do not know anything on this topic. Therefore, if this is necessary, I will need additional time to figure it out ...

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Resolving "The LocalizedControlType of ImageAlign/TextAlign/Anchor/Dock property is inconsistent in Inspect" for Anchor property - @DmitryGorokhov
item.description:
Fixes #4102

Proposed changes

  • Rework LocalizedControlType of Anchor properties
  • Added unit tests

Customer Impact

  • No

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • Minimal

Screenshots

Before

The LocalizedControlType of following property is inconsistent in Inspect:

  • For ImageAlign & TextAlign property, it's radio button.
  • For Anchor property, it's panel.
  • For Dock property, it's check box.

After

ImageAlign & TextAlign

Nothing changes, because they work like a RadioButton and it's already radio button.

Anchor

The LocalizedControlType of Anchor is check box now. The DefaultAction is Check or Uncheck, and IsInvokePatternAvailable is true. Anchor control is pronounced correctly by Narrator (like Top check box checked or unchecked).

Dock

Not resolved for DockEditor due difficulties fixing mistakes appear in narrator announcement for this control. The new issue is should be created to fix LocalizedControlType and other properties of the Dock.

In addiction

I don't change base of DockEditorCheckBox from CheckBox to RadioButton. I found comment in the code: "Even though the selections are mutually exclusive, I'm using CheckBoxes instead of RadioButtons because RadioButtons fire Click events whenever they get focus, which is bad when the user is trying to tab to a specific control using the keyboard.". So I've decided to check this. If we change base of DockEditorCheckBox to RadioButton then the panel with options opens and closes fastly when we try to change Dock. So we have not able to change Dock value using special button.

GIF

Mistake was reported by CTI (if we try to rework Dock as a RadioButton control)

Narrator-Regression: the narrator can't announce the status for Dock property. More info: After compared between Dock and ImageAlign properties., due to they are all RaidoButton type, found there have some properties are different.

Test methodology

  • Unit tests
  • Inspect
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

item.title: PR: Fixing wrong color for label in high contrast mode (port to 5.0) - @SergeySmirnov-Akvelon
item.description:
Fixes #5196

Proposed changes

  • Ported fix for .NET Framework 4.8 (changeset)
  • This code was added to the .NET Framework, but later removed due to an (792810) issue. Unfortunately, this line remains in the .NET Core 3.1 and next versions, so we see this bug.

Regression?

  • Yes (from .NET Core 3.1)

Risk

  • Minimal

Test methodology

  • Manually
  • CTI team

Test environment(s)

Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19041.388] .NET Core SDK: 6.0.0-preview.7.21352.2

Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow

Regression?

Risk

  • Minimal

Test methodology

  • CTI team

item.title: PR: Implementing invoke action of UpDownButton - @DmitryGorokhov
item.description:
Proposed changes

  • Overrided DoDefaultAction method in DirectionButtonAccessibleObject class
  • Added unit tests
  • Added init items list for domainUpDown1 placed on MultipleControls form

Customer Impact

  • The invoke action of UpDownButtons will be work correctly

Regression?

  • No

Risk

  • Minimal

Screenshots

Before

If you trigger the Invoke action, the value is not updated

After

You can see correct action result now

Fix after testing

The LegacyIAccessible.DefaultAction property value is "Press" (not null) now:

Test methodology

  • Manually
  • Unit tests
  • Accessibility tests

Accessibility testing

  • Inspect
  • Accessibility Insights

Test environment(s)

  • .NET SDK: Version: 6.0.0-preview.7.21358.2
Microsoft Reviewers: Open in CodeFlow
